title: HardInterventionsCommute
tex_block_html: Let $G:=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG and $W_1, W_2 \ins J \cup V$ two subsets of nodes from $G$. Then we have: \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} = \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)} = G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}. \]

tex_statement.raw:
% Claim statement file for row claim_3_4 -- "HardInterventionsCommute".
% Auto-generated stub by scaffold/solve_chapter.py at row start. The
% manager agent (or a worker it dispatches) fills the body below with the
% claim's statement(s) from the lecture notes -- statement only, no proof
% (proofs live in the sibling `claim_3_4_proof_*.tex` file).
%
% This file is a sibling of `main.tex` inside `<subsection>/tex/`. The
% `subfiles` package lets it render both standalone and as part of
% `main.tex`. Note: `main.tex` does NOT \subfile this statement file -- the
% sibling `_proof_` file restates the statement above the proof, so
% including both in `main.tex` would be redundant. This file exists for
% standalone reading / grep convenience.

\documentclass[main]{subfiles}

\begin{document}
% ref: claim_3_4 (statement)
% title: HardInterventionsCommute
\def\rowref{claim\_3\_4}
\phantomsection\label{claim_3_4}

\begin{Lem}[Hard interventions commute]\label{hard-interventions-commute}
    Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG (def \ref{def-cdmg}); throughout we use the notation of def \ref{not-cdmg} and recall the typing and axioms of def \ref{def-cdmg}, namely $J \cap V = \emptyset$, $E \subseteq (J \cup V) \times V$, $L \subseteq V \times V$, $L$ irreflexive ($(v_1, v_2) \in L \Rightarrow v_1 \neq v_2$), and $L$ symmetric ($(v_1, v_2) \in L \Leftrightarrow (v_2, v_1) \in L$). Let
    \[ W_1 \subseteq J \cup V \quad\text{and}\quad W_2 \subseteq J \cup V \]
    be two subsets of nodes (no disjointness between $W_1$ and $W_2$ is assumed, and overlap with $J$ is permitted, as per def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention}'s precondition). All three quantifiers ``for every CDMG $G$'', ``for every $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$'', and ``for every $W_2 \subseteq J \cup V$'' are read \emph{universally}.

    \emph{Carrier matching for the iterated hard interventions.} The inner two terms of the equality below are iterated hard interventions of the form $(G_{\doit(W_i)})_{\doit(W_j)}$, in which $\doit(W_j)$ is applied to the CDMG $G_{\doit(W_i)}$ produced by def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention}. For this outer application to be well-typed per def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention}'s precondition ``$W \subseteq J \cup V$ of the input CDMG'', we need $W_j$ to be a subset of the carrier of $G_{\doit(W_i)}$, i.e.\
    \[ W_j \;\subseteq\; J_{\doit(W_i)} \cup V_{\doit(W_i)}. \]
    This holds because hard intervention preserves the carrier $J \cup V$ as a set: by items i.\ and ii.\ of def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention},
    \[
        J_{\doit(W_i)} \cup V_{\doit(W_i)} \;=\; (J \cup W_i) \cup (V \sm W_i) \;=\; J \cup V,
    \]
    where the second equality uses $W_i \subseteq J \cup V$ (as in the carrier-matching paragraph of \ref{claim_3_3}). Hence $W_j \subseteq J \cup V = J_{\doit(W_i)} \cup V_{\doit(W_i)}$, so $(G_{\doit(W_i)})_{\doit(W_j)}$ is defined. The same observation applied to $W_1 \cup W_2 \subseteq J \cup V$ shows that the right-hand side $G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$ is also defined per def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention}.

    \emph{The conclusion.} The CDMGs $(G_{\doit(W_1)})_{\doit(W_2)}$, $(G_{\doit(W_2)})_{\doit(W_1)}$, and $G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$ (all constructed via def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} as just verified) coincide. Concretely, the LN's displayed triple equality
    \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} = \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)} = G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)} \]
    is the conjunction of the two following equalities of CDMGs:
    \begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)]
        \item \emph{Composition with $W_1$ then $W_2$.}
              \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} \;=\; G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}. \]
        \item \emph{Composition with $W_2$ then $W_1$.}
              \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)} \;=\; G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}. \]
    \end{enumerate}
    Together (a) and (b) yield the LN's ``symmetry under swap'' reading $\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} = \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}$ by transitivity of equality. The equality of CDMGs in (a) and (b) is understood componentwise on the four components $(J, V, E, L)$ of def \ref{def-cdmg}, but the conjunctive unpacking into the four field-by-field equalities is deferred to the proof.

      %This shows that our notion:
      %\[ G(V \sm (W_1 \cup W_2) | \doit(W_1 \cup W_2 \cup J))   \]
      %is unambiguous.
\end{Lem}

\end{document}
tex_statement.html:
Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG (def def-cdmg); throughout we use the notation of def not-cdmg and recall the typing and axioms of def def-cdmg, namely $J \cap V = \emptyset$, $E \subseteq (J \cup V) \times V$, $L \subseteq V \times V$, $L$ irreflexive ($(v_1, v_2) \in L \Rightarrow v_1 \neq v_2$), and $L$ symmetric ($(v_1, v_2) \in L \Leftrightarrow (v_2, v_1) \in L$). Let \[ W_1 \subseteq J \cup V \quad\text{and}\quad W_2 \subseteq J \cup V \] be two subsets of nodes (no disjointness between $W_1$ and $W_2$ is assumed, and overlap with $J$ is permitted, as per def def:G_hard_intervention's precondition). All three quantifiers ŌĆ£for every CDMG $G$ŌĆØ, ŌĆ£for every $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$ŌĆØ, and ŌĆ£for every $W_2 \subseteq J \cup V$ŌĆØ are read universally.

Carrier matching for the iterated hard interventions. The inner two terms of the equality below are iterated hard interventions of the form $(G_{\doit(W_i)})_{\doit(W_j)}$, in which $\doit(W_j)$ is applied to the CDMG $G_{\doit(W_i)}$ produced by def def:G_hard_intervention. For this outer application to be well-typed per def def:G_hard_intervention's precondition ŌĆ£$W \subseteq J \cup V$ of the input CDMGŌĆØ, we need $W_j$ to be a subset of the carrier of $G_{\doit(W_i)}$, i.e.\ \[ W_j \;\subseteq\; J_{\doit(W_i)} \cup V_{\doit(W_i)}. \] This holds because hard intervention preserves the carrier $J \cup V$ as a set: by items i.\ and ii.\ of def def:G_hard_intervention, \[ J_{\doit(W_i)} \cup V_{\doit(W_i)} \;=\; (J \cup W_i) \cup (V \sm W_i) \;=\; J \cup V, \] where the second equality uses $W_i \subseteq J \cup V$ (as in the carrier-matching paragraph of claim_3_3). Hence $W_j \subseteq J \cup V = J_{\doit(W_i)} \cup V_{\doit(W_i)}$, so $(G_{\doit(W_i)})_{\doit(W_j)}$ is defined. The same observation applied to $W_1 \cup W_2 \subseteq J \cup V$ shows that the right-hand side $G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$ is also defined per def def:G_hard_intervention.

The conclusion. The CDMGs $(G_{\doit(W_1)})_{\doit(W_2)}$, $(G_{\doit(W_2)})_{\doit(W_1)}$, and $G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$ (all constructed via def def:G_hard_intervention as just verified) coincide. Concretely, the LN's displayed triple equality \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} = \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)} = G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)} \] is the conjunction of the two following equalities of CDMGs:

  1. Composition with $W_1$ then $W_2$. \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} \;=\; G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}. \]
  2. Composition with $W_2$ then $W_1$. \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)} \;=\; G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}. \]

Together (a) and (b) yield the LN's ŌĆ£symmetry under swapŌĆØ reading $\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} = \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}$ by transitivity of equality. The equality of CDMGs in (a) and (b) is understood componentwise on the four components $(J, V, E, L)$ of def def-cdmg, but the conjunctive unpacking into the four field-by-field equalities is deferred to the proof.

% Claim proof file for row claim_3_4 -- "HardInterventionsCommute".
% Refactor twin of `claim_3_4_proof_HardInterventionsCommute.tex` for the
% `cdmg_typed_edges` refactor.  The LN statement is unchanged; the proof
% body is identical to the original on the (J, V, E) components, and the
% L-component paragraph plus its closing "Remark on the Lean encoding of
% L" are revised to reflect the post-refactor `Sym2 Node` encoding of L
% (under which the previously-registered deviation
% hard_intervention_l_symmetrized_removal is structurally resolved at the
% def_3_10 row).
%
% This file is a sibling of `main.tex` inside `<subsection>/tex/`. The
% `subfiles` package lets it render both standalone and as part of
% `main.tex` via `\subfile{...}`.
%
% The statement is restated above the proof so the file is self-contained
% when rendered on its own. The proof must:
%   - Stay close to the lecture notes' proof if one exists (always search
%     the LN first for a `\begin{proof}` block immediately following the
%     claim; copy / lightly edit when present).
%   - Otherwise use the LN paradigm and cite prior claims by their `ref`.
%   - Be self-contained enough that a mathematician can follow it without
%     re-reading the LN.
%   - Have no `sorry`, `omitted`, or "obvious" shortcuts.

\documentclass[main]{subfiles}

\begin{document}
% ref: claim_3_4 (proof, with statement restated for readability)
% title: HardInterventionsCommute
\def\rowref{claim\_3\_4}
\phantomsection\label{claim_3_4}

% --- statement (restated) ---------------------------------------------------
\begin{Lem}[Hard interventions commute]\label{hard-interventions-commute}
    Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG (def \ref{def-cdmg}); throughout we use the notation of def \ref{not-cdmg} and recall the typing and axioms of def \ref{def-cdmg}, namely $J \cap V = \emptyset$, $E \subseteq (J \cup V) \times V$, $L \subseteq V \times V$, $L$ irreflexive ($(v_1, v_2) \in L \Rightarrow v_1 \neq v_2$), and $L$ symmetric ($(v_1, v_2) \in L \Leftrightarrow (v_2, v_1) \in L$). Let
    \[ W_1 \subseteq J \cup V \quad\text{and}\quad W_2 \subseteq J \cup V \]
    be two subsets of nodes (no disjointness between $W_1$ and $W_2$ is assumed, and overlap with $J$ is permitted, as per def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention}'s precondition). All three quantifiers ``for every CDMG $G$'', ``for every $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$'', and ``for every $W_2 \subseteq J \cup V$'' are read \emph{universally}.

    \emph{Carrier matching for the iterated hard interventions.} The inner two terms of the equality below are iterated hard interventions of the form $(G_{\doit(W_i)})_{\doit(W_j)}$, in which $\doit(W_j)$ is applied to the CDMG $G_{\doit(W_i)}$ produced by def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention}. For this outer application to be well-typed per def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention}'s precondition ``$W \subseteq J \cup V$ of the input CDMG'', we need $W_j$ to be a subset of the carrier of $G_{\doit(W_i)}$, i.e.\
    \[ W_j \;\subseteq\; J_{\doit(W_i)} \cup V_{\doit(W_i)}. \]
    This holds because hard intervention preserves the carrier $J \cup V$ as a set: by items i.\ and ii.\ of def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention},
    \[
        J_{\doit(W_i)} \cup V_{\doit(W_i)} \;=\; (J \cup W_i) \cup (V \sm W_i) \;=\; J \cup V,
    \]
    where the second equality uses $W_i \subseteq J \cup V$ (as in the carrier-matching paragraph of \ref{claim_3_3}). Hence $W_j \subseteq J \cup V = J_{\doit(W_i)} \cup V_{\doit(W_i)}$, so $(G_{\doit(W_i)})_{\doit(W_j)}$ is defined. The same observation applied to $W_1 \cup W_2 \subseteq J \cup V$ shows that the right-hand side $G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$ is also defined per def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention}.

    \emph{The conclusion.} The CDMGs $(G_{\doit(W_1)})_{\doit(W_2)}$, $(G_{\doit(W_2)})_{\doit(W_1)}$, and $G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$ (all constructed via def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} as just verified) coincide. Concretely, the LN's displayed triple equality
    \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} = \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)} = G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)} \]
    is the conjunction of the two following equalities of CDMGs:
    \begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)]
        \item \emph{Composition with $W_1$ then $W_2$.}
              \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} \;=\; G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}. \]
        \item \emph{Composition with $W_2$ then $W_1$.}
              \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)} \;=\; G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}. \]
    \end{enumerate}
    Together (a) and (b) yield the LN's ``symmetry under swap'' reading $\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} = \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}$ by transitivity of equality. The equality of CDMGs in (a) and (b) is understood componentwise on the four components $(J, V, E, L)$ of def \ref{def-cdmg}, but the conjunctive unpacking into the four field-by-field equalities is deferred to the proof.

      %This shows that our notion:
      %\[ G(V \sm (W_1 \cup W_2) | \doit(W_1 \cup W_2 \cup J))   \]
      %is unambiguous.
\end{Lem}

% --- proof ------------------------------------------------------------------
% Proof body adapted from the LN's \Claude{...} block at
% lecture-notes/lecture_notes/graphs.tex lines 326-355, with three light edits:
% (i) the opening is re-framed to reference the rewrite's (a) and (b) labels,
% (ii) the closing "by symmetry" remark for (b) is expanded into an explicit
% W_1 <-> W_2 swap + commutativity-of-union step, and
% (iii) the bidirected-edge paragraph carries an inline note that, under the
% cdmg_typed_edges refactor's `Sym2 Node` encoding of L, the previously-
% registered deviation `hard_intervention_l_symmetrized_removal` is
% structurally resolved at the def_3_10 row (no symmetrisation step in either
% encoding is needed at this proof's L-component step).
\begin{proof}
We prove sub-claim (a), $\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} = G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$, by checking the equality of the two CDMGs componentwise on each of the four components $(J, V, E, L)$ of def \ref{def-cdmg} in turn; sub-claim (b), $\lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)} = G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$, is then obtained by an explicit symmetry argument at the end of the proof. Both sub-claims rely on the carrier-matching observation already established in the statement block above to license the outer $\doit(\cdot)$ application.

\medskip\noindent\textbf{Proof of (a): composition with $W_1$ then $W_2$.}
We verify the equality $\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} = G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$ on each of the four components.

\medskip\noindent\emph{Input nodes.}
By item i.\ of def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} applied twice (first to $G$ with $W_1$, then to $G_{\doit(W_1)}$ with $W_2$),
\[
    J_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)}} \;=\; J_{\doit(W_1)} \cup W_2 \;=\; (J \cup W_1) \cup W_2 \;=\; J \cup (W_1 \cup W_2) \;=\; J_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)},
\]
where the middle equality is associativity of $\cup$. \checkmark

\medskip\noindent\emph{Output nodes.}
By item ii.\ of def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} applied twice,
\[
    V_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)}} \;=\; V_{\doit(W_1)} \sm W_2 \;=\; (V \sm W_1) \sm W_2 \;=\; V \sm (W_1 \cup W_2) \;=\; V_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)},
\]
where the middle equality is the standard identity $(A \sm B) \sm C = A \sm (B \cup C)$ for set difference (an element $x$ survives both removals iff $x \notin B$ and $x \notin C$, iff $x \notin B \cup C$). \checkmark

\medskip\noindent\emph{Directed edges.}
By item iii.\ of def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} applied twice,
\begin{align*}
    E_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)}}
    &= E_{\doit(W_1)} \sm \lC v \tuh w \mid w \in W_2 \rC \\
    &= \lp E \sm \lC v \tuh w \mid w \in W_1 \rC \rp
       \sm \lC v \tuh w \mid w \in W_2 \rC \\
    &= E \sm \lC v \tuh w \mid w \in W_1 \cup W_2 \rC
     = E_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}.
\end{align*}
The penultimate equality is the same set-difference identity used for the output nodes, instantiated at the directed-edge set: an ordered pair $(v, w) \in E$ survives both removal steps iff its head $w$ lies neither in $W_1$ nor in $W_2$, iff $w \notin W_1 \cup W_2$; that condition is exactly the one matching the removal set of a single application of item iii.\ at the union $W_1 \cup W_2$. \checkmark

\medskip\noindent\emph{Bidirected edges.}
By item iv.\ of def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} applied twice,
\begin{align*}
    L_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)}}
    &= L_{\doit(W_1)} \sm \lC v \huh w \mid w \in W_2 \rC \\
    &= \lp L \sm \lC v \huh w \mid w \in W_1 \rC \rp
       \sm \lC v \huh w \mid w \in W_2 \rC \\
    &= L \sm \lC v \huh w \mid w \in W_1 \cup W_2 \rC
     = L_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}.
\end{align*}
The penultimate equality combines the two removal clauses by the same union-of-conditions step used for the directed-edge case, with $v \huh w$ in place of $v \tuh w$. \checkmark

\smallskip\noindent\emph{Remark on the Lean encoding of $L$ under the \texttt{cdmg\_typed\_edges} refactor.} Under the post-refactor Lean encoding of def \ref{def-cdmg} (the \texttt{cdmg\_typed\_edges} refactor; see \texttt{leanification/refactors/refactor\_cdmg\_typed\_edges.md} for the design block), $L$ is typed as a $\Finset$ of \emph{unordered} pairs $\Sym_2(\Node)$ rather than as a $\Finset$ of ordered pairs $\Node \times \Node$ paired with a separate $\Sym$-symmetry axiom. Consequently, item iv.\ of def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} reads literally: a bidirected edge $s \in L$ (an unordered pair) is removed iff some endpoint $v$ of $s$ lies in $W$ (kept-condition: $\forall v \in s,\ v \notin W$). There is no ``second component'' on an unordered pair to test in isolation, so the LN's literal one-sided phrasing $v_2 \in W$ collapses to ``any endpoint in $W$'', and no symmetrisation step is required to satisfy any CDMG axiom (swap-symmetry is definitional on $\Sym_2$, so $s(v_1, v_2) = s(v_2, v_1)$ holds by construction). The previously-registered deviation \emph{hard\_intervention\_l\_symmetrized\_removal} -- attached to the pre-refactor encoding of def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} -- is therefore \emph{structurally resolved at the def\_3\_10 row} under the refactor, and does not surface at this row. The composition step of this proof, expressed at the Lean level, reads as ``filter-by-$p_1$ then filter-by-$p_2 = $ filter-by-$(p_1 \wedge p_2)$'' with $p_i(s) := (\forall v \in s,\ v \notin W_i)$: applied at the union $W_1 \cup W_2$, the pointwise predicate equivalence
\[
    (\forall v \in s,\ v \notin W_1) \wedge (\forall v \in s,\ v \notin W_2) \;\Longleftrightarrow\; \forall v \in s,\ v \notin W_1 \cup W_2
\]
follows by pulling the conjunction inside the bounded universal (each disjoint-$v$ instance is one $\wedge$-pair) plus the membership-in-union rewrite $v \notin W_1 \cup W_2 \Leftrightarrow v \notin W_1 \wedge v \notin W_2$. Both the LN's set-difference algebra in the displayed line and the Lean-level composition step land on the same final bidirected-edge set $L_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$.

\smallskip
Combining the four componentwise equalities above, the CDMGs $\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)}$ and $G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$ agree field by field on each of $(J, V, E, L)$ and are therefore equal as CDMGs (def \ref{def-cdmg}). This establishes sub-claim (a).

\medskip\noindent\textbf{Proof of (b): composition with $W_2$ then $W_1$.}
Sub-claim (b) is obtained by swapping the roles of $W_1$ and $W_2$ in the argument for (a). Concretely, re-running the four-component check above with the substitution $W_1 \leftrightarrow W_2$ -- the proof depends on the pair of subsets only through their symbolic roles, and the displayed identities $(J \cup W_1) \cup W_2 = J \cup (W_1 \cup W_2)$, $(V \sm W_1) \sm W_2 = V \sm (W_1 \cup W_2)$, and the analogous edge-set identities all retain their truth under the swap -- yields
\[
    \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)} \;=\; G_{\doit(W_2 \cup W_1)}.
\]
This is precisely sub-claim (a) instantiated at the swapped pair $(W_2, W_1)$. We emphasise that sub-claim (b) is \emph{not} a literal instance of (a): the iterated hard intervention on the left-hand side of (b) is built from the swapped pair $(W_2, W_1)$, and the right-hand-side union appearing in (a) for that swapped pair is $W_2 \cup W_1$, not the $W_1 \cup W_2$ in the statement of (b). The two right-hand sides agree by commutativity of union, $W_2 \cup W_1 = W_1 \cup W_2$, which gives
\[
    G_{\doit(W_2 \cup W_1)} \;=\; G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}
\]
as CDMGs: def \ref{def:G_hard_intervention} depends on $W$ only through the underlying set $W \subseteq J \cup V$ (each of items i.\ -- iv.\ refers to $W$ only via the operations $J \cup W$, $V \sm W$, and the membership predicate ``$\cdot \in W$''), and equal sets produce equal CDMGs componentwise. Chaining the two equalities,
\[
    \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)} \;=\; G_{\doit(W_2 \cup W_1)} \;=\; G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)},
\]
establishes sub-claim (b).

\medskip\noindent\emph{Recovery of the LN's triple equality.}
By sub-claims (a) and (b) -- both of which equate their respective iterated hard intervention with the single right-hand side $G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$ -- transitivity of equality on that shared right-hand side yields
\[
    \lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} \;=\; G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)} \;=\; \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)},
\]
which is the LN's stated triple equality and in particular recovers the swap-symmetry reading $\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} = \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}$.
\end{proof}

\end{document}
tex_proof.html:
Lemma ŌĆö Hard interventions commute

Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG (def def-cdmg); throughout we use the notation of def not-cdmg and recall the typing and axioms of def def-cdmg, namely $J \cap V = \emptyset$, $E \subseteq (J \cup V) \times V$, $L \subseteq V \times V$, $L$ irreflexive ($(v_1, v_2) \in L \Rightarrow v_1 \neq v_2$), and $L$ symmetric ($(v_1, v_2) \in L \Leftrightarrow (v_2, v_1) \in L$). Let \[ W_1 \subseteq J \cup V \quad\text{and}\quad W_2 \subseteq J \cup V \] be two subsets of nodes (no disjointness between $W_1$ and $W_2$ is assumed, and overlap with $J$ is permitted, as per def def:G_hard_intervention's precondition). All three quantifiers ŌĆ£for every CDMG $G$ŌĆØ, ŌĆ£for every $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$ŌĆØ, and ŌĆ£for every $W_2 \subseteq J \cup V$ŌĆØ are read universally.

Carrier matching for the iterated hard interventions. The inner two terms of the equality below are iterated hard interventions of the form $(G_{\doit(W_i)})_{\doit(W_j)}$, in which $\doit(W_j)$ is applied to the CDMG $G_{\doit(W_i)}$ produced by def def:G_hard_intervention. For this outer application to be well-typed per def def:G_hard_intervention's precondition ŌĆ£$W \subseteq J \cup V$ of the input CDMGŌĆØ, we need $W_j$ to be a subset of the carrier of $G_{\doit(W_i)}$, i.e.\ \[ W_j \;\subseteq\; J_{\doit(W_i)} \cup V_{\doit(W_i)}. \] This holds because hard intervention preserves the carrier $J \cup V$ as a set: by items i.\ and ii.\ of def def:G_hard_intervention, \[ J_{\doit(W_i)} \cup V_{\doit(W_i)} \;=\; (J \cup W_i) \cup (V \sm W_i) \;=\; J \cup V, \] where the second equality uses $W_i \subseteq J \cup V$ (as in the carrier-matching paragraph of claim_3_3). Hence $W_j \subseteq J \cup V = J_{\doit(W_i)} \cup V_{\doit(W_i)}$, so $(G_{\doit(W_i)})_{\doit(W_j)}$ is defined. The same observation applied to $W_1 \cup W_2 \subseteq J \cup V$ shows that the right-hand side $G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$ is also defined per def def:G_hard_intervention.

The conclusion. The CDMGs $(G_{\doit(W_1)})_{\doit(W_2)}$, $(G_{\doit(W_2)})_{\doit(W_1)}$, and $G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$ (all constructed via def def:G_hard_intervention as just verified) coincide. Concretely, the LN's displayed triple equality \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} = \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)} = G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)} \] is the conjunction of the two following equalities of CDMGs:

  1. Composition with $W_1$ then $W_2$. \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} \;=\; G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}. \]
  2. Composition with $W_2$ then $W_1$. \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)} \;=\; G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}. \]

Together (a) and (b) yield the LN's ŌĆ£symmetry under swapŌĆØ reading $\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} = \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}$ by transitivity of equality. The equality of CDMGs in (a) and (b) is understood componentwise on the four components $(J, V, E, L)$ of def def-cdmg, but the conjunctive unpacking into the four field-by-field equalities is deferred to the proof.

Proof.

We prove sub-claim (a), $\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} = G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$, by checking the equality of the two CDMGs componentwise on each of the four components $(J, V, E, L)$ of def def-cdmg in turn; sub-claim (b), $\lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)} = G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$, is then obtained by an explicit symmetry argument at the end of the proof. Both sub-claims rely on the carrier-matching observation already established in the statement block above to license the outer $\doit(\cdot)$ application.

Proof of (a): composition with $W_1$ then $W_2$. We verify the equality $\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} = G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$ on each of the four components.

Input nodes. By item i.\ of def def:G_hard_intervention applied twice (first to $G$ with $W_1$, then to $G_{\doit(W_1)}$ with $W_2$), \[ J_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)}} \;=\; J_{\doit(W_1)} \cup W_2 \;=\; (J \cup W_1) \cup W_2 \;=\; J \cup (W_1 \cup W_2) \;=\; J_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}, \] where the middle equality is associativity of $\cup$. Ō£ō

Output nodes. By item ii.\ of def def:G_hard_intervention applied twice, \[ V_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)}} \;=\; V_{\doit(W_1)} \sm W_2 \;=\; (V \sm W_1) \sm W_2 \;=\; V \sm (W_1 \cup W_2) \;=\; V_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}, \] where the middle equality is the standard identity $(A \sm B) \sm C = A \sm (B \cup C)$ for set difference (an element $x$ survives both removals iff $x \notin B$ and $x \notin C$, iff $x \notin B \cup C$). Ō£ō

Directed edges. By item iii.\ of def def:G_hard_intervention applied twice, \[\begin{aligned} E_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)}} &= E_{\doit(W_1)} \sm \lC v \tuh w \mid w \in W_2 \rC \\ &= \lp E \sm \lC v \tuh w \mid w \in W_1 \rC \rp \sm \lC v \tuh w \mid w \in W_2 \rC \\ &= E \sm \lC v \tuh w \mid w \in W_1 \cup W_2 \rC = E_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}. \end{aligned}\] The penultimate equality is the same set-difference identity used for the output nodes, instantiated at the directed-edge set: an ordered pair $(v, w) \in E$ survives both removal steps iff its head $w$ lies neither in $W_1$ nor in $W_2$, iff $w \notin W_1 \cup W_2$; that condition is exactly the one matching the removal set of a single application of item iii.\ at the union $W_1 \cup W_2$. Ō£ō

Bidirected edges. By item iv.\ of def def:G_hard_intervention applied twice, \[\begin{aligned} L_{\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)}} &= L_{\doit(W_1)} \sm \lC v \huh w \mid w \in W_2 \rC \\ &= \lp L \sm \lC v \huh w \mid w \in W_1 \rC \rp \sm \lC v \huh w \mid w \in W_2 \rC \\ &= L \sm \lC v \huh w \mid w \in W_1 \cup W_2 \rC = L_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}. \end{aligned}\] The penultimate equality combines the two removal clauses by the same union-of-conditions step used for the directed-edge case, with $v \huh w$ in place of $v \tuh w$. Ō£ō

Remark on the Lean encoding of $L$ under the cdmg_typed_edges refactor. Under the post-refactor Lean encoding of def def-cdmg (the cdmg_typed_edges refactor; see leanification/refactors/refactor_cdmg_typed_edges.md for the design block), $L$ is typed as a $\Finset$ of unordered pairs $\Sym_2(\Node)$ rather than as a $\Finset$ of ordered pairs $\Node \times \Node$ paired with a separate $\Sym$-symmetry axiom. Consequently, item iv.\ of def def:G_hard_intervention reads literally: a bidirected edge $s \in L$ (an unordered pair) is removed iff some endpoint $v$ of $s$ lies in $W$ (kept-condition: $\forall v \in s,\ v \notin W$). There is no ŌĆ£second componentŌĆØ on an unordered pair to test in isolation, so the LN's literal one-sided phrasing $v_2 \in W$ collapses to ŌĆ£any endpoint in $W$ŌĆØ, and no symmetrisation step is required to satisfy any CDMG axiom (swap-symmetry is definitional on $\Sym_2$, so $s(v_1, v_2) = s(v_2, v_1)$ holds by construction). The previously-registered deviation hard\_intervention\_l\_symmetrized\_removal ŌĆō attached to the pre-refactor encoding of def def:G_hard_intervention ŌĆō is therefore structurally resolved at the def\_3\_10 row under the refactor, and does not surface at this row. The composition step of this proof, expressed at the Lean level, reads as ŌĆ£filter-by-$p_1$ then filter-by-$p_2 = $ filter-by-$(p_1 \wedge p_2)$ŌĆØ with $p_i(s) := (\forall v \in s,\ v \notin W_i)$: applied at the union $W_1 \cup W_2$, the pointwise predicate equivalence \[ (\forall v \in s,\ v \notin W_1) \wedge (\forall v \in s,\ v \notin W_2) \;\Longleftrightarrow\; \forall v \in s,\ v \notin W_1 \cup W_2 \] follows by pulling the conjunction inside the bounded universal (each disjoint-$v$ instance is one $\wedge$-pair) plus the membership-in-union rewrite $v \notin W_1 \cup W_2 \Leftrightarrow v \notin W_1 \wedge v \notin W_2$. Both the LN's set-difference algebra in the displayed line and the Lean-level composition step land on the same final bidirected-edge set $L_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$.

Combining the four componentwise equalities above, the CDMGs $\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)}$ and $G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$ agree field by field on each of $(J, V, E, L)$ and are therefore equal as CDMGs (def def-cdmg). This establishes sub-claim (a).

Proof of (b): composition with $W_2$ then $W_1$. Sub-claim (b) is obtained by swapping the roles of $W_1$ and $W_2$ in the argument for (a). Concretely, re-running the four-component check above with the substitution $W_1 \leftrightarrow W_2$ ŌĆō the proof depends on the pair of subsets only through their symbolic roles, and the displayed identities $(J \cup W_1) \cup W_2 = J \cup (W_1 \cup W_2)$, $(V \sm W_1) \sm W_2 = V \sm (W_1 \cup W_2)$, and the analogous edge-set identities all retain their truth under the swap ŌĆō yields \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)} \;=\; G_{\doit(W_2 \cup W_1)}. \] This is precisely sub-claim (a) instantiated at the swapped pair $(W_2, W_1)$. We emphasise that sub-claim (b) is not a literal instance of (a): the iterated hard intervention on the left-hand side of (b) is built from the swapped pair $(W_2, W_1)$, and the right-hand-side union appearing in (a) for that swapped pair is $W_2 \cup W_1$, not the $W_1 \cup W_2$ in the statement of (b). The two right-hand sides agree by commutativity of union, $W_2 \cup W_1 = W_1 \cup W_2$, which gives \[ G_{\doit(W_2 \cup W_1)} \;=\; G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)} \] as CDMGs: def def:G_hard_intervention depends on $W$ only through the underlying set $W \subseteq J \cup V$ (each of items i.\ ŌĆō iv.\ refers to $W$ only via the operations $J \cup W$, $V \sm W$, and the membership predicate ŌĆ£$\cdot \in W$ŌĆØ), and equal sets produce equal CDMGs componentwise. Chaining the two equalities, \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)} \;=\; G_{\doit(W_2 \cup W_1)} \;=\; G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}, \] establishes sub-claim (b).

Recovery of the LN's triple equality. By sub-claims (a) and (b) ŌĆō both of which equate their respective iterated hard intervention with the single right-hand side $G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)}$ ŌĆō transitivity of equality on that shared right-hand side yields \[ \lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} \;=\; G_{\doit(W_1 \cup W_2)} \;=\; \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}, \] which is the LN's stated triple equality and in particular recovers the swap-symmetry reading $\lp G_{\doit(W_1)} \rp_{\doit(W_2)} = \lp G_{\doit(W_2)} \rp_{\doit(W_1)}$.

lean_blocks.explanation: This is a `variable` declaration: Lean auto-binds `Node : Type*` and the instance `[DecidableEq Node]` as implicit arguments to every subsequent declaration in the surrounding `CDMG` namespace, sparing us from rewriting the same parameters on `subset_carrier_of_hardInterventionOn` and on `hardInterventionsCommute`. The `Type*` notation makes `Node` universe-polymorphic, so callers can instantiate it at any universe level. The `DecidableEq Node` assumption is load-bearing for this row in particular: the statement references `Finset.union` (forming `G.J Ōł¬ W` and `WŌéü Ōł¬ WŌéé`), `Finset.sdiff` (the `\` in `G.V \ W`), and `Finset.filter` (used inside `hardInterventionOn` and reasoned about via `Finset.filter_filter` and `Finset.filter_congr` in the proof) ŌĆö all of which need decidable equality on the element type to compute the resulting `Finset`. Stronger structure on `Node` (e.g.\ `Fintype`, `LinearOrder`) is not required here and is deferred to downstream consumers that actually use it. Same convention as the rest of `Chapter3_GraphTheory`'s files.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Auto-binds `Node` + `DecidableEq Node` as implicits; decidability drives `Finset.union`/`sdiff`/`filter` used below.
variable {Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]

lean_blocks.explanation: This is a `variable` declaration: Lean auto-binds `Node : Type*` and the instance `[DecidableEq Node]` as implicit arguments to every subsequent declaration in the surrounding `CDMG` namespace, sparing us from rewriting the same parameters on `subset_carrier_of_hardInterventionOn` and on `hardInterventionsCommute`. The `Type*` notation makes `Node` universe-polymorphic, so callers can instantiate it at any universe level. The `DecidableEq Node` assumption is load-bearing for this row in particular: the statement references `Finset.union` (forming `G.J Ōł¬ W` and `WŌéü Ōł¬ WŌéé`), `Finset.sdiff` (the `\` in `G.V \ W`), and `Finset.filter` (used inside `hardInterventionOn` and reasoned about via `Finset.filter_filter` and `Finset.filter_congr` in the proof) ŌĆö all of which need decidable equality on the element type to compute the resulting `Finset`. Stronger structure on `Node` (e.g.\ `Fintype`, `LinearOrder`) is not required here and is deferred to downstream consumers that actually use it. Same convention as the rest of `Chapter3_GraphTheory`'s files.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Auto-binds `Node` + `DecidableEq Node` as implicits; decidability drives `Finset.union`/`sdiff`/`filter` used below.
variable {Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]

lean_blocks.code:
private lemma subset_carrier_of_hardInterventionOn
    {G : CDMG Node} {W : Finset Node} (hW : W ŌŖå G.J Ōł¬ G.V)
    {S : Finset Node} (hS : S ŌŖå G.J Ōł¬ G.V) :
    S ŌŖå (G.hardInterventionOn W hW).J Ōł¬
          (G.hardInterventionOn W hW).V
lean_blocks.explanation: This private helper transports a carrier-subset hypothesis `S ŌŖå G.J Ōł¬ G.V` across one application of `hardInterventionOn`, producing `S ŌŖå (G.hardInterventionOn W hW).J Ōł¬ (G.hardInterventionOn W hW).V`. It exists because the main theorem's nested `(...).hardInterventionOn WŌéé ?_` needs a subset proof against the *inner-intervened* CDMG's carrier, not against `G`'s original carrier. The proof is pure set algebra: the inner carrier unfolds to `(G.J Ōł¬ W) Ōł¬ (G.V \ W)`, which equals `G.J Ōł¬ G.V` as a set, so any element of `S` lands somewhere. The `L`-component of `G` is never touched here, so the `Sym2 Node` encoding of `L` is structurally invisible ŌĆö the helper body is the same shape under either L-encoding. Implicit `G`, `W`, `S` and explicit `hW`, `hS` mirror `hardInterventionOn`'s binder convention, so the call site reads `subset_carrier_of_hardInterventionOn hWŌéü hWŌéé` left-to-right as "inner intervention on `WŌéü`; transported set is `WŌéé`". `private` localises the lemma to this file.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Private helper: transport a carrier-subset hypothesis across one `hardInterventionOn`.
private lemma subset_carrier_of_hardInterventionOn
    -- Implicit `G` (the CDMG) and `W` (the intervention set), with `hW` the
    -- precondition that `W` sits inside `G`'s carrier `G.J Ōł¬ G.V`.
    {G : CDMG Node} {W : Finset Node} (hW : W ŌŖå G.J Ōł¬ G.V)
    -- Implicit `S` (the transported set), with `hS` placing it in `G`'s carrier.
    {S : Finset Node} (hS : S ŌŖå G.J Ōł¬ G.V) :
    -- Conclusion: `S` also sits inside the post-intervention carrier
    -- `(G.J Ōł¬ W) Ōł¬ (G.V \ W)`, which equals `G.J Ōł¬ G.V` as a set.
    S ŌŖå (G.hardInterventionOn W hW).J Ōł¬
          (G.hardInterventionOn W hW).V

lean_blocks.code:
theorem hardInterventionsCommute (G : CDMG Node)
    (WŌéü WŌéé : Finset Node)
    (hWŌéü : WŌéü ŌŖå G.J Ōł¬ G.V) (hWŌéé : WŌéé ŌŖå G.J Ōł¬ G.V) :
    (G.hardInterventionOn WŌéü hWŌéü).hardInterventionOn WŌéé
        (subset_carrier_of_hardInterventionOn hWŌéü hWŌéé)
      = G.hardInterventionOn (WŌéü Ōł¬ WŌéé) (Finset.union_subset hWŌéü hWŌéé)
    Ōł¦
    (G.hardInterventionOn WŌéé hWŌéé).hardInterventionOn WŌéü
        (subset_carrier_of_hardInterventionOn hWŌéé hWŌéü)
      = G.hardInterventionOn (WŌéü Ōł¬ WŌéé) (Finset.union_subset hWŌéü hWŌéé)
lean_blocks.explanation: This is the row's main theorem: hard interventions commute, packaged as the conjunction of two binary CDMG equalities. The conjunct `.1` is sub-claim (a) ŌĆö composing `WŌéü` then `WŌéé` equals intervening on `WŌéü Ōł¬ WŌéé` in one shot. The conjunct `.2` is sub-claim (b), the symmetric statement with the two intervention sets swapped. The LN's swap-symmetry reading `(G_{do(WŌéü)})_{do(WŌéé)} = (G_{do(WŌéé)})_{do(WŌéü)}` is recovered by `.1.trans .2.symm`. The nested intervention on the LHS of each conjunct discharges its inner-`hW` obligation via `subset_carrier_of_hardInterventionOn`, and the RHS's union-subset obligation via the Mathlib one-liner `Finset.union_subset`. No disjointness or `W Ōł® G.J = Ōłģ` hypothesis is imposed ŌĆö the union on the right handles overlap correctly. The signature's binder shape `(G) (WŌéü WŌéé) (hWŌéü hWŌéé)` directly echoes `hardInterventionOn`'s `(W) (hW)` shape replicated for the two intervention sets, so call sites parse identically here and at every downstream consumer.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Main theorem: hard interventions commute, packaged as conjunction (a) Ōł¦ (b).
theorem hardInterventionsCommute (G : CDMG Node)
    -- Two intervention sets, named `WŌéü` and `WŌéé` to match the LN's $W_1, W_2$.
    (WŌéü WŌéé : Finset Node)
    -- Each `WßĄó` lies in `G`'s carrier; `hWßĄó` is the precondition `hardInterventionOn` consumes.
    (hWŌéü : WŌéü ŌŖå G.J Ōł¬ G.V) (hWŌéé : WŌéé ŌŖå G.J Ōł¬ G.V) :
    -- Sub-claim (a): intervening on `WŌéü` then `WŌéé` equals intervening on the union.
    (G.hardInterventionOn WŌéü hWŌéü).hardInterventionOn WŌéé
        -- Inner-`hW` for the nested intervention: transport `hWŌéé` across the `WŌéü`-intervention.
        (subset_carrier_of_hardInterventionOn hWŌéü hWŌéé)
      -- RHS uses `Finset.union_subset` to combine `hWŌéü` and `hWŌéé` into `WŌéü Ōł¬ WŌéé ŌŖå G.J Ōł¬ G.V`.
      = G.hardInterventionOn (WŌéü Ōł¬ WŌéé) (Finset.union_subset hWŌéü hWŌéé)
    -- Conjunction with the symmetric sub-claim.
    Ōł¦
    -- Sub-claim (b): swapped order ŌĆö `WŌéé` then `WŌéü` ŌĆö equals the same union.
    (G.hardInterventionOn WŌéé hWŌéé).hardInterventionOn WŌéü
        -- Same transport helper, with `hWŌéü` and `hWŌéé` swapped to match the swapped nesting.
        (subset_carrier_of_hardInterventionOn hWŌéé hWŌéü)
      -- RHS identical to (a) ŌĆö both sub-claims equate to the same `WŌéü Ōł¬ WŌéé` intervention.
      = G.hardInterventionOn (WŌéü Ōł¬ WŌéé) (Finset.union_subset hWŌéü hWŌéé)

design_choices:
**One theorem returning `(a) Ōł¦ (b)`, not two separate top-level theorems.** The LN packages its triple equality $(G_{\mathrm{do}(W_1)})_{\mathrm{do}(W_2)} = (G_{\mathrm{do}(W_2)})_{\mathrm{do}(W_1)} = G_{\mathrm{do}(W_1 \cup W_2)}$ in a single lemma; the rewritten canonical statement decomposes it into two binary equalities. Lean has no native triple-equality syntax, so a single theorem returning `(a) Ōł¦ (b)` is the literal rendering. Consumers reach `.1` and `.2`; the swap-symmetry reading is recovered as `.1.trans .2.symm`. Two top-level theorems were rejected because they would duplicate the antecedents `hWŌéü, hWŌéé` at both heads.

**No disjointness hypothesis; overlap with `G.J` permitted.** The LN reads "two subsets of nodes" with no rider. The lemma is a *free composition* result: `hardInterventionOn` commutes with itself unconditionally, and `WŌéü Ōł¬ WŌéé` on the right handles overlap. A disjointness binder would strictly weaken the statement.

**Carrier-subset transport via the private helper `subset_carrier_of_hardInterventionOn`, not an inline `by`-block.** The nested `(...).hardInterventionOn WŌéé ?_` needs a subset proof against the inner-intervened CDMG's carrier. Inlining would bloat the rendered statement and duplicate the carrier-matching reasoning.

**`L` is `Finset (Sym2 Node)`; the `hard_intervention_l_symmetrized_removal` deviation is structurally resolved upstream at `def_3_10`.** The LN's item iv. collapses to the kept-condition `fun s : Sym2 Node => ŌłĆ v Ōłł s, v Ōłē W`. No symmetrisation is required ŌĆö `s(v, w) = s(w, v)` is definitional on `Sym2`. The L-branches therefore carry no "Remark on the Lean encoding of L" bridging paragraph.

**A local `private instance hardInterventionsCommute_decidable_bAll` replicates the `def_3_10`-site `DecidablePred` instance.** The def-site instance is `private` and does not propagate by `import`; the `change` step in the L-branch forces `DecidablePred` synthesis here. Body is the same `Sym2.recOnSubsingleton` + `Sym2.ball` reduction. Promoting the def-site instance to public would impose the reduction on every importer; inline `classical` would tank kernel performance.

**Proof skeleton: `Finset.filter_filter` + `Finset.filter_congr`, with one `Finset.union_comm` for sub-claim (b).** Componentwise: J via `Finset.union_assoc`; V via `sdiff_sdiff_left`; E and L via `filter_filter` + `filter_congr` against `(pŌéü Ōł¦ pŌéé) Ōåö pŌéüŌéé` (cashed out by `Finset.mem_union, not_or` for E, and the `forall_and`-distributed `Sym2`-quotient analogue for L). Sub-claim (b)'s extra `Finset.union_comm` is the tex's "by symmetry" closing step made literal.

**Inline `cdmgExt` extensionality, not a top-level `@[ext]` lemma.** Eight fields are destructured by `rintro` plus four `obtain rfl`; the `hL_symm` field is absent since swap-symmetry is definitional on `Sym2`. This is the first row to need componentwise CDMG equality, so a single-use local lemma keeps the proof self-contained.
